what_it_does: Lists six JSX canvas component files under the claude-ds project as an allocation batch for wave-002, batch-004 of the interpret pipeline.

Every path is under 'app/canvases/' and uses a PascalCase filename ending in .jsx
The file contains no code symbols or content; it is purely a roster
The batch path suggests a staged allocation inside 'wave-002/alloc/batch-004'
standouts: Entire batch consists of canvas components (Bridge, Build, Colors, Components, Icons, Imagery)
No symbols[] are provided even though the listed files are code components
conventions: Uses a JSON array of uniform objects with project/path/root keys
Component filenames use PascalCase and live in an 'app/canvases/' directory
Batch files are organized by wave/alloc/batch hierarchy
concerns: Absolute root path '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds' is user-specific and not portable
No indication of why these six files are grouped together in this batch
No version, timestamp, or status metadata
Symbols array is empty, so the file does not carry any extracted code facts
notes: This is a minimal allocation roster that assigns six canvas JSX files to interpret wave-002 batch-004 without any extracted symbols.
questions: What criteria determine which files belong to batch-004?

intention_signals: It appears to schedule a specific set of design-system canvas JSX components for a wave of interpretation or fusion processing.
contribution: Provides an authoritative roster of which canvas component paths belong to interpret wave-002, batch-004, which a fused system would keep as the batch membership record.
